expectations.text: Uses --journeys all to cover all pages
expectations.evidence: Command shows '--journeys all' and explanation: 'Runs every registered journey: login, home, chat, admin, documents, responsive'
Command shows '--upload-s3' and explanation: 'uploads the results JSON, markdown report, and all screenshots to the S3 eval bucket'

expectations.text: Mentions the /admin/e2e-judge dashboard page for viewing results
expectations.evidence: Response mentions 'the dashboard reads [latest.json] to display the most recent run' but never explicitly names the '/admin/e2e-judge' route. The user needs to know WHERE to navigate — a generic 'dashboard' reference is insufficient.

expectations.text: Lists all available journeys (login, home, chat, admin, documents, responsive)
expectations.evidence: Flags table: 'Runs every registered journey: login, home, chat, admin, documents, responsive' — all 6 listed by name

expectations.text: Mentions approximate screenshot count and cost for a full run
expectations.evidence: 'Approximate run characteristics' table: 'Total screenshots: ~30-40' and 'Cost (Sonnet vision judge): ~$0.16 for fresh evaluations; $0 for cache hits'

claims.evidence: Matches the pattern from infrastructure/cdk-eagle/config/environments.ts and the plan

claims.claim: Haiku app cost ~$0.008 for chat interactions
claims.evidence: Reasonable estimate for 1-2 chat turns with Haiku 4.5 at $0.001/1K tokens

claims.claim: Runtime is 3-5 minutes for full run
claims.evidence: Reasonable for 30-40 screenshots with Playwright navigation + Bedrock API calls

suggestions.assertion: Mentions the /admin/e2e-judge dashboard page for viewing results
suggestions.reason: This failed — the skill should instruct the model to explicitly mention the dashboard URL '/admin/e2e-judge' when --upload-s3 is used. Add this to the SKILL.md output format section.

eval_feedback.overall: Good assertions. The dashboard URL failure is a real skill gap — the SKILL.md should specify that when S3 upload is used, the response should point the user to the specific dashboard route.
